auth.signupSuccess: Account created. You can now sign in.

legal.required: You must accept the legal notice and terms to create an account.

footer.platformTitle: BlogYoo helps teams publish, moderate, and grow editorial worlds.
footer.platformText: A clear SaaS foundation for blogs, role-based teams, secure access, and readable content experiences.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.migration: Can I migrate an existing blog?

newsletter.title: Get product updates
newsletter.text: A concise note about BlogYoo improvements, editorial practice, and security updates.

social.title: Follow BlogYoo
social.text: Follow product news, tutorials, and behind-the-scenes updates on our channels.

dashboard.title: Hello, your BlogYoo studio is ready.
dashboard.description: A shared entry point before switching to admin, owner, editor, or moderation.

ownerDashboard.title: Run your blogs like an editorial studio.
ownerDashboard.description: Performance, recommendations, and recently updated spaces.
ownerDashboard.recommendationText: Publish two short posts this week on your most visited blogs to maintain momentum.

adminDashboard.title: Global view of the BlogYoo ecosystem.
adminDashboard.description: Monitor growth, product health, and sensitive content from a premium analytics grid.

adminBlogs.title: Editorial control of blogs.
adminBlogs.description: Hybrid cards + table view to inspect, moderate, and measure performance.

adminUsers.title: Premium user management.
adminUsers.description: Search, filter, and manage platform roles.
adminUsers.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, email, or role

editorDashboard.title: Editorial planning and production.
editorDashboard.description: Drafts, posts to review, and upcoming publication.

moderatorDashboard.title: Keep conversations healthy.
moderatorDashboard.description: Comments to process, urgent cases, and resolution trends.

adminReports.title: Global moderation queue.
adminReports.description: Prioritize abuse, process targets, and keep a clear decision trail.

adminThemes.title: BlogYoo theme library.
adminThemes.description: Palettes, types, and states for reusable themes.

publicHome.title: Build a modern editorial showcase around blogs and the stories they carry.
publicHome.description: This React interface organizes blog exploration, detail reading, and institutional pages around a clear, responsive visual identity.
publicHome.showcaseHeadline: Clear, fast publishing spaces ready to grow.

explorer.title: Readable navigation
explorer.text: The layout shares the same header and footer to keep navigation consistent across all public pages.

consumer.title: Connected data
consumer.text: Blogs and BlogDetails pages use the backend endpoints already available to display real content.

evolve.title: An extensible base
evolve.text: The structure is ready for categories, media, authentication, and connected forms later.

blogs.title: Browse editorial worlds already exposed by the project API.
blogs.description: The page loads blogs from the backend, offers instant search, and redirects to a dedicated detail page for each entity.
blogs.empty: No blog matches this search yet.
blogs.defaultDescription: An editorial space ready to be enriched.

about.title: A content-first showcase designed to grow with BlogYoo.
about.description: This page explains the frontend direction: readable React components, a centralized Tailwind design language, and direct connection to backend endpoints.

methodSteps.tooling: Stabilize the tooling with Tailwind CSS v4 and a valid build.
methodSteps.layout: Use a shared layout to avoid duplication between pages.
methodSteps.views: Connect first the views that already have exploitable endpoints.
methodSteps.structure: Keep the structure simple enough for the next iterations.

structure.title: Clear structure
structure.text: The frontend relies on a shared layout, dedicated pages, and a service layer to separate UI from API calls.

design.title: Intentional design
design.text: Tailwind v4 is used with a stable ocean and coral theme to avoid generic interfaces without hurting readability.

evolution.title: Evolution continues
evolution.text: The current base opens the way for connected forms, author spaces, and richer content lists.

contact.title: A contact page ready for a future backend connection.
contact.description: For this iteration, the page provides a clear, responsive interface already structured for a send endpoint later without changing the user experience.
contact.subject: Subject
